terms.definition: A named storage location with a type and a value.
The compiled form of Java source code that the JVM runs.
Explicitly converting a value to another compatible type.
The remainder left after integer division, written with %.
Putting one control structure inside another.
&& or || stopping as soon as the result is already known.
The numeric position of an item in an array or list.
Arrays whose same indexes describe related information.
The <> in new ArrayList<>() that lets Java infer the generic type.
A variable in a method header that receives a value.
A value or expression supplied when calling a method.
Using the same method name with different parameter lists.
A blueprint that defines fields and methods.
An instance created from a class.
A variable that belongs to an object or class.
A no-argument constructor; Java can supply one only when none is declared.
A constructor that receives another object of the same type and copies its state.
A record constructor that accepts one value for each record component.
One parent or interface reference using behavior from different object types.
A subclass supplying its own version of an inherited method.
A method with no body that a concrete subclass must implement.
A contract listing behavior a class must provide.
The keyword used when a class agrees to an interface contract.
A Java interface for keyboard pressed, released, and typed events.
Putting a value into a variable using =.
Two references pointing to the same object, tested with ==.
A method that returns a String representation of an object.
An object representing a problem during program execution.
An exception from using a null reference as if it were an object.
A try form that closes a resource such as Scanner automatically.
One shared field that belongs to the class.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: A method that subclasses cannot override.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: Unused objects kept reachable by references, preventing cleanup.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: The object that provides drawing methods in Swing.
A request for Swing to redraw a component later.
A position, usually x then y, in the drawing area.
A compact declaration for an immutable-style data carrier.
A method that returns a record component, such as name().
An object mainly used to hold related data.
Choosing an overridden instance method based on the actual object at runtime.
A method changing outside state, printing, or doing I/O.
An unfinished method kept in place while a program is being built.
A reserved Java word such as class, public, or return.
Statements enclosed by braces { }.
Joining Strings with +.
